label: Multimodal foundation models

node.label: Multimodal foundation models
node.definition: A multimodal foundation model aligns and jointly processes text, images, audio, video, or actions through shared token spaces and cross-modal interaction.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Multimodal foundation models is to connect Transformers, Cross-attention & co-attention to downstream capabilities including Multimodal assistants & vision-language systems, Browser, desktop & computer-use agents, Robotics & embodied AI.
examples: substrate for Multimodal assistants & vision-language systems
substrate for Browser, desktop & computer-use agents
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Transformers, Cross-attention & co-attention.

dependencies.rationale: Multimodal foundation models extends or specializes the architecture family Transformers.
Multimodal foundation models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Cross-attention & co-attention.
Multimodal foundation models is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Contrastive and mutual-information objectives.
Multimodal foundation models consumes, emits, or organizes information using Multimodal, simulated & streaming data.
Multimodal foundation models extends or specializes the architecture family VQ-VAE & learned discrete tokenizers.
